Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value.

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")

# Create a simple Python detection function
def has_python(language_desc):
    if not language_desc:
        return False
    return 'python' in language_desc.lower()

# Test on some samples
print("\n=== Testing Python detection ===")
if languages_sample:
    for i, repo in enumerate(languages_sample[:5]):
        desc = repo.get('language_description', '')
        print(f"Repo {i+1}: {repo['repo_name'][:40]}")
        print(f"  Has Python: {has_python(desc)}")
        print(f"  Desc: {desc[:80]}...")
        print()

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")

import json
import os

# Load the Python repos data from the JSON file
python_file_key = 'var_functions.query_db:32'
python_file = locals().get(python_file_key)

if python_file and isinstance(python_file, str) and '.json' in python_file:
    with open(python_file, 'r') as f:
        python_repos = json.load(f)
else:
    python_repos = python_file if python_file else []

print(f"Loaded {len(python_repos)} Python repos from {python_file_key}")

# Load the general languages sample
languages_sample = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:30')
print(f"Languages sample size: {len(languages_sample) if languages_sample else 0}")

# Load the README sample
readme_sample = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:24')
print(f"README sample size: {len(readme_sample) if readme_sample else 0}")

# Create a simple Python detection function
def has_python(language_desc):
    if not language_desc:
        return False
    return 'python' in language_desc.lower()

# Test on some samples
print("\n=== Testing Python detection ===")
if languages_sample:
    for i, repo in enumerate(languages_sample[:5]):
        desc = repo.get('language_description', '')
        print(f"Repo {i+1}: {repo['repo_name'][:40]}")
        print(f"  Has Python: {has_python(desc)}")
        print(f"  Desc: {desc[:80]}...")
        print()

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

import json
import re

# Load non-Python repos from file
non_python_file = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:38')
if non_python_file and isinstance(non_python_file, str) and '.json' in non_python_file:
    with open(non_python_file, 'r') as f:
        non_python_repos = json.load(f)
else:
    non_python_repos = non_python_file if non_python_file else []

print(f'Loaded {len(non_python_repos)} non-Python repositories')

# Extract just the repo names
repo_names = [repo['repo_name'] for repo in non_python_repos[:100]]

# Print a few examples
print('Sample repo names:')
for i, name in enumerate(repo_names[:5]):
    print(f'  {i+1}: {name}')

# Create a string for SQL IN clause (simplified)
repo_list_str = ', '.join([f'"{repo}"' for repo in repo_names[:10]])
print(f'\nFirst 10 repos for query: {repo_list_str}')

# Save the full list for next query
result = {
    'total_repos': len(repo_names),
    'sample_repos': repo_names[:20]
}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)"}, {"sample_repo_name": "DynamoRIO/dynamorio", "sample_path": "api/docs/README.md", "content": "This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API

"}, {"sample_repo_name": "ninja-ide/ninja-ide", "sample_path": "README.md", "content": "![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")

"}, {"sample_repo_name": "sclorg/rhscl-dockerfiles", "sample_path": "centos7.mongodb24/examples/replica/README.md", "content": "# MongoDB Replica Set example

**WARNING: This is only a Proof-Of-Concept example and it is not meant to be used in
production. Use at your own risk.**

## What is a MongoDB replica set?

A cluster of MongoDB servers that implements master-slave replication and automated failover.
MongoDB’s recommended replication strategy.

## Deployment

To create a MongoDB replica set in OpenShift v3, you can use the template
included in this repository and create a new deployment right away:

```
$ oc new-app 2.4/examples/replica/mongodb-clustered.json
```

Since the provided template uses ephemeral storage, after every redeploy you get
an empty database, and **all data from a previous deploy is lost**.

## How does it work?

### Service 'mongodb'

This resource defines a [headless service](http://kubernetes.io/v1.0/docs/user-guide/services.html#headless-services)
that serves as an entry point to the replica set. The service endpoints are
the pods created by the 'mongodb' DeploymentConfig.

A headless service allows using DNS queries to discover other MongoDB
endpoints from inside the container, by querying the service name (e.g.: `dig
mongodb A +short +search`).

### DeploymentConfig 'mongodb'

This resource defines a [deployment configuration](https://docs.openshift.org/latest/architecture/core_concepts/deployments.html#deployments-and-deployment-configurations) to manage replica set members.
Each member starts the MongoDB server without replication data. Once it is
ready, it advertises itself to the current replica set PRIMARY, which will then
add it to the replica set.
When a member pod is destroyed, it is automatically removed from the replica set.

To add/remove members to the replica set you can use the `oc scale` command.
This will scale the deployment up to 5 pods:

```
$ oc scale dc mongodb --replicas=5
```

The provided template will start three replicas by default.
MongoDB recommends using an [odd number of replicas](http://docs.mongodb.org/v2.4/tutorial/deploy-replica-set/#overview).

#### Post-deployment hook

The DeploymentConfig has a post-deployment hook that runs once after every
deploy, and is responsible for initializing the replica set, as well as creating
the database and users. The hook terminates after the initialization is complete,
a PRIMARY is elected and data is replicated to other members of the replica set.

## Configuration

There are a few environment variables that can be used to configure the
replica set, all of them have default values.

* `MONGODB_REPLICA_NAME` - name of the replica set (default: `rs0`).
* `MONGODB_SERVICE_NAME` - name of the MongoDB service (default: `mongodb`, used by DNS lookup).
* `MONGODB_ADMIN_PASSWORD` - password for the `admin` user (roles: 'dbAdminAnyDatabase', 'userAdminAnyDatabase', 'readWriteAnyDatabase', 'clusterAdmin') (default: *generated*).
* `MONGODB_DATABASE` - name of the database (default: `userdb`)
* `MONGODB_USER` - the name of the regular MongoDB user (roles: 'readWrite' for `$MONGODB_DATABASE`) (default: *generated*).
* `MONGODB_PASSWORD` - the regular MongoDB user password (default: *generated*).
* `MONGODB_KEYFILE_VALUE` - value for '[keyFile](http://docs.mongodb.org/v2.4/tutorial/generate-key-file/)' file that MongoDB members use for authorization internally (default: *generated*).

The patterns for generated values are defined in the template parameters.

## Teardown

For deleting all the created resources use:

```
$ oc delete all --all
```
"}, {"sample_repo_name": "cwilso/midi-synth", "sample_path": "README.md", "content": "# MIDI Synth

This application is a analog synthesizer simulation built on the [Web Audio API](https://dvcs.w3.org/hg/audio/raw-file/tip/webaudio/specification.html).  It is very loosely based on the architecture of a [Moog Prodigy](http://www.vintagesynth.com/moog/prodigy.php) synthesizer, although this is a polyphonic synthesizer, and it lacks the oscillator sync and glide effects of the Prodigy.  (AKA: this is not intended to be a replication of the Prodigy, so pleased don't tell me how crappy a reproduction it is! :)

This uses my [Web MIDI Polyfill](https://github.com/cwilso/WebMIDIAPIShim) to add MIDI support via the [Web MIDI API](http://webaudio.github.io/web-midi-api/) - in fact, I partly wrote this as a test case for the polyfill and the MIDI API itself, so if you have a MIDI keyboard attached, check it out.  The polyfill uses Java to access the MIDI device, so if you're wondering why Java is loading, that's why.  It may take a few seconds for MIDI to become active - the library takes a while to load - but when the ring turns gray (instead of blue), it's ready.  If you have a native implementation of the Web MIDI API in your browser, the polyfill shouldn't load - at the time of this writing, Chrome Stable (from version 43) has the only such implementation. Earlier versions of Chrome (from version 33) can enable Web MIDI via chrome://flags/#enable-web-midi

You can try it out live at https://webaudiodemos.appspot.com/midi-synth/index.html.

Check it out, feel free to fork, submit pull requests, etc.

-Chris

    'sample_repos': readme_repo_names[:20]
}

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load README data file
readme_file = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:50')
if readme_file and isinstance(readme_file, str) and '.json' in readme_file:
    with open(readme_file, 'r') as f:
        readme_data = json.load(f)
else:
    readme_data = readme_file if readme_file else []

print('Loaded %d README files from artifacts database' % len(readme_data))

# Load non-Python repos count
non_python_count_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:52')
total_non_python_repos = 0
if non_python_count_data:
    total_non_python_repos = non_python_count_data[0]['non_python_repo_count']

print('Total non-Python repositories: %d' % total_non_python_repos)

# Extract unique repo names from READMEs
readme_repo_names = list(set([item['sample_repo_name'] for item in readme_data]))
print('Unique repositories in README sample: %d' % len(readme_repo_names))

# Check a few REA DME contents for copyright
print('\nLooking for copyright information in some READMEs:')
copyright_pattern = re.compile(r'(copyright|©|\(c\))', re.IGNORECASE)
copyright_count = 0
for i, item in enumerate(readme_data[:20]):
    content = item.get('content', '')
    has_copyright = bool(copyright_pattern.search(content))
    if has_copyright:
        copyright_count += 1
    print('README %d (repo: %s): copyright=%s' % (i+1, item['sample_repo_name'], has_copyright))

print('\nCopyright count in first 20 READMEs: %d' % copyright_count)

result = {
    'total_readmes': len(readme_data),
    'unique_repos_in_readmes': len(readme_repo_names),
    'total_non_python_repos': total_non_python_repos,
    'copyright_in_first_20': copyright_count
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

# Load non-Python repos count
non_python_count_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:52')

Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

"}, {"repo_name": "sclorg/rhscl-dockerfiles", "content": "# MongoDB Replica Set example

**WARNING: This is only a Proof-Of-Concept example and it is not meant to be used in
production. Use at your own risk.**

## What is a MongoDB replica set?

A cluster of MongoDB servers that implements master-slave replication and automated failover.
MongoDB’s recommended replication strategy.

## Deployment

To create a MongoDB replica set in OpenShift v3, you can use the template
included in this repository and create a new deployment right away:

```
$ oc new-app 2.4/examples/replica/mongodb-clustered.json
```

Since the provided template uses ephemeral storage, after every redeploy you get
an empty database, and **all data from a previous deploy is lost**.

## How does it work?

### Service 'mongodb'

This resource defines a [headless service](http://kubernetes.io/v1.0/docs/user-guide/services.html#headless-services)
that serves as an entry point to the replica set. The service endpoints are
the pods created by the 'mongodb' DeploymentConfig.

A headless service allows using DNS queries to discover other MongoDB
endpoints from inside the container, by querying the service name (e.g.: `dig
mongodb A +short +search`).

### DeploymentConfig 'mongodb'

This resource defines a [deployment configuration](https://docs.openshift.org/latest/architecture/core_concepts/deployments.html#deployments-and-deployment-configurations) to manage replica set members.
Each member starts the MongoDB server without replication data. Once it is
ready, it advertises itself to the current replica set PRIMARY, which will then
add it to the replica set.
When a member pod is destroyed, it is automatically removed from the replica set.

To add/remove members to the replica set you can use the `oc scale` command.
This will scale the deployment up to 5 pods:

```
$ oc scale dc mongodb --replicas=5
```

The provided template will start three replicas by default.
MongoDB recommends using an [odd number of replicas](http://docs.mongodb.org/v2.4/tutorial/deploy-replica-set/#overview).

#### Post-deployment hook

The DeploymentConfig has a post-deployment hook that runs once after every
deploy, and is responsible for initializing the replica set, as well as creating
the database and users. The hook terminates after the initialization is complete,
a PRIMARY is elected and data is replicated to other members of the replica set.

## Configuration

There are a few environment variables that can be used to configure the
replica set, all of them have default values.

* `MONGODB_REPLICA_NAME` - name of the replica set (default: `rs0`).
* `MONGODB_SERVICE_NAME` - name of the MongoDB service (default: `mongodb`, used by DNS lookup).
* `MONGODB_ADMIN_PASSWORD` - password for the `admin` user (roles: 'dbAdminAnyDatabase', 'userAdminAnyDatabase', 'readWriteAnyDatabase', 'clusterAdmin') (default: *generated*).
* `MONGODB_DATABASE` - name of the database (default: `userdb`)
* `MONGODB_USER` - the name of the regular MongoDB user (roles: 'readWrite' for `$MONGODB_DATABASE`) (default: *generated*).
* `MONGODB_PASSWORD` - the regular MongoDB user password (default: *generated*).
* `MONGODB_KEYFILE_VALUE` - value for '[keyFile](http://docs.mongodb.org/v2.4/tutorial/generate-key-file/)' file that MongoDB members use for authorization internally (default: *generated*).

The patterns for generated values are defined in the template parameters.

## Teardown

For deleting all the created resources use:

```
$ oc delete all --all
```
"}, {"repo_name": "cwilso/midi-synth", "content": "# MIDI Synth

This application is a analog synthesizer simulation built on the [Web Audio API](https://dvcs.w3.org/hg/audio/raw-file/tip/webaudio/specification.html).  It is very loosely based on the architecture of a [Moog Prodigy](http://www.vintagesynth.com/moog/prodigy.php) synthesizer, although this is a polyphonic synthesizer, and it lacks the oscillator sync and glide effects of the Prodigy.  (AKA: this is not intended to be a replication of the Prodigy, so pleased don't tell me how crappy a reproduction it is! :)

This uses my [Web MIDI Polyfill](https://github.com/cwilso/WebMIDIAPIShim) to add MIDI support via the [Web MIDI API](http://webaudio.github.io/web-midi-api/) - in fact, I partly wrote this as a test case for the polyfill and the MIDI API itself, so if you have a MIDI keyboard attached, check it out.  The polyfill uses Java to access the MIDI device, so if you're wondering why Java is loading, that's why.  It may take a few seconds for MIDI to become active - the library takes a while to load - but when the ring turns gray (instead of blue), it's ready.  If you have a native implementation of the Web MIDI API in your browser, the polyfill shouldn't load - at the time of this writing, Chrome Stable (from version 43) has the only such implementation. Earlier versions of Chrome (from version 33) can enable Web MIDI via chrome://flags/#enable-web-midi

You can try it out live at https://webaudiodemos.appspot.com/midi-synth/index.html.

Check it out, feel free to fork, submit pull requests, etc.

-Chris
"}, {"repo_name": "pezy/LeetCode", "content": "这道题，我昨天就在想了。毕竟平衡搜索树只不过是特殊的平衡二叉树罢了。是否平衡的关键在于题目里给出的这个定义：
> a height-balanced binary tree is defined as a binary tree in which the depth of the two subtrees of every node never differ by more than 1.

所以这个问题很重要的一个子问题就是：如何求二叉树的高度。假设我们找到了高度的方法，那么核心判断就是：
```cpp
std::abs(height(root->left), height(root->right)) <= 1
```

所以我几乎是瞬间写出了[解决方法](../../e6906ef932bda154ad17518e6d097c904045048f/22.%20Balanced%20Binary%20Tree/solution.h#L22-L32)\uff0

WITH non_python_repos AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT l.repo_name 
  FROM metadata_database.languages l 
  WHERE l.language_description NOT LIKE '%python%'
  LIMIT 1000
)
SELECT c.sample_repo_name as repo_name, c.content
FROM contents c
WHERE (c.sample_path = 'README.md' OR c.sample_path LIKE '%/README.md')
  AND c.sample_repo_name IN (SELECT repo_name FROM non_python_repos)
LIMIT 100

import json
import re

# Load README data
readme_file = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:60')
if readme_file and isinstance(readme_file, str) and '.json' in readme_file:
    with open(readme_file, 'r') as f:
        readme_data = json.load(f)
else:
    readme_data = readme_file if readme_file else []

print('Loaded %d README files' % len(readme_data))

# Extract unique repo names
readme_repos = list(set(item['repo_name'] for item in readme_data))
print('Found %d unique repositories' % len(readme_repos))

# Sample of repos to check
print('First 5 repos to check: %s' % str(readme_repos[:5]))

# Save repos for next query
with open('/tmp/readme_repos.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(readme_repos, f)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'readme_repos_count': len(readme_repos), 'sample_repos': readme_repos[:10]}))

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")

import json
import re

# Load README data
readme_file = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:60')
if readme_file and isinstance(readme_file, str) and '.json' in readme_file:
    with open(readme_file, 'r') as f:
        readme_data = json.load(f)
else:
    readme_data = readme_file if readme_file else []

print('Loaded %d README files' % len(readme_data))

# Extract unique repo names
readme_repos = list(set(item['repo_name'] for item in readme_data))
print('Found %d unique repositories' % len(readme_repos))

# Sample of repos to check
print('First 5 repos to check: %s' % str(readme_repos[:5]))

# Save repos for next query
with open('/tmp/readme_repos.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(readme_repos, f)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'readme_repos_count': len(readme_repos), 'sample_repos': readme_repos[:10]}))

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

args.code:
import json

# Load the list of repos with READMEs
with open('/tmp/readme_repos.json', 'r') as f:
    readme_repos = json.load(f)

print('Number of repos with READMEs to check: %d' % len(readme_repos))

# Create a string for SQL IN clause (we need to batch this due to size limitations)
# Let's use a temporary table approach or batch processing
# For now, let's just save the list

with open('/tmp/readme_repos_filtered.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(readme_repos, f)

print('First 10 repos:')
for i, repo in enumerate(readme_repos[:10]):
    print('  %d: %s' % (i+1, repo))

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'repos_to_check': len(readme_repos)}))

Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

and usage documentation, found at

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus

## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*

-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?

import json

# Load the list of repos with READMEs
with open('/tmp/readme_repos.json', 'r') as f:
    readme_repos = json.load(f)

print('Number of repos with READMEs to check: %d' % len(readme_repos))

# Create a string for SQL IN clause (we need to batch this due to size limitations)
# Let's use a temporary table approach or batch processing
# For now, let's just save the list

with open('/tmp/readme_repos_filtered.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(readme_repos, f)

print('First 10 repos:')
for i, repo in enumerate(readme_repos[:10]):
    print('  %d: %s' % (i+1, repo))

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'repos_to_check': len(readme_repos)}))

Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

import json

# Load the README data
readme_file = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:60')
if readme_file and isinstance(readme_file, str) and '.json' in readme_file:
    with open(readme_file, 'r') as f:
        readme_data = json.load(f)
else:
    readme_data = readme_file if readme_file else []

# Extract repo names
readme_repos = [item['repo_name'] for item in readme_data]

print('Found %d READMEs from %d unique repositories' % (len(readme_data), len(set(readme_repos))))

# Load the language data for these repos from previous query
lang_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:74')

# Build a map of repo -> has_python
python_repos = set()
for item in lang_data:
    desc = item.get('language_description', '')
    if desc and 'python' in desc.lower():
        python_repos.add(item['repo_name'])

print('\nPython repos found: %d' % len(python_repos))

# Filter READMEs to only non-Python repos
non_python_readmes = []
for item in readme_data:
    if item['repo_name'] not in python_repos:
        non_python_readmes.append(item)

print('Non-Python READMEs: %d' % len(non_python_readmes))

# Save non-Python READMEs for copyright analysis
with open('/tmp/non_python_readmes.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(non_python_readmes, f)

print('\nFirst 5 non-Python repos:')
for i, item in enumerate(non_python_readmes[:5]):
    print('  %d: %s' % (i+1, item['repo_name']))

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_readmes': len(readme_data),
    'python_repos_with_readmes': len(python_repos),
    'non_python_readmes': len(non_python_readmes)
}))

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*

var_functions.query_db:24.content:
Script for use with Casper's Self Service when deploying Canon printers. Script checks **/Library/Printers/Canon/CUPSPS2/Utilities/Canon CUPS PS Printer Utility.app/Contents/Info.plist** for the **CFBundleVersion** key value. 

If the value returned is less than the version of the current drivers, the print drivers are installed by a Casper policy before the requested printer is set up. 

If the installed drivers are the same version or higher as the print drivers available on the Casper server, this information is logged and the Canon print drivers are not installed.

Blog post that uses this script is available here:

[http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/](http://derflounder.wordpress.com/2014/02/06/deploying-canon-print-drivers-with-printer-setups-via-caspers-self-service/)

This directory contains the doxygen source for the DynamoRIO public API
and usage documentation, found at
[dynamorio.org](http://dynamorio.org/docs/index.html)

![Travis report](https://travis-ci.org/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.svg?branch=master "Travis-C.I. Testing report")

# Ninja-ide Is Not Just Another IDE.
**Ninja-IDE** is a cross-platform integrated development environment (IDE) that allows developers to create applications for any purpose making the task of writing software easier and more enjoyable. It's also a secret ninja agency but this doesn't matter right now.
![Ninja-IDE logo](http://ninjaide.webfactional.com/static/common/img/ninja-big.png)


## Platforms
- Linux/X11
- Mac OS X
- Windows
- BSD


## Ninja contact
-   [Ninja website](http://ninja-ide.org "http://ninja-ide.org") at ninja-ide.org
-   [Mailing List](http://groups.google.com/group/ninja-ide/topics "Ninja Google Groups") at Google Groups
-   [@ninja\\_ide](https://twitter.com/ninja_ide "@ninja_ide") at Twitter
-   [+Ninja-IDE](https://plus.google.com/103973182574871451647 "Ninja-IDE at Google Plus") at Google Plus
-   [Ninja-IDE](https://kiwiirc.com/client/chat.freenode.net/?nick=Ninja%7C?&theme=cli#ninja-ide "ninja-ide at Freenode.net") at Freenode.net


## Requirements
On any system you want **Ninja-IDE**, you'll need to have this dependencies installed:

-   [Python](http://python.org "Python Homepage") >= 2.7  *(or Python3)*
-   [PyQt4](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/pyqt/intro "PyQt Homepage") >= 4.8  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PyQt4-QScintilla2](http://www.riverbankcomputing.com/software/qscintilla/intro "QScintilla2 Homepage") >= 2.0  *(Not Qt5)*
-   [PIP](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/installing.html "About Installing PIP")  *(Not PIP3)*
-   [Virtualenv](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/virtualenv "About Installing Virtualenv")


## Installing on Mac OS
```bash
brew install qt pyqt sip qscintilla2
```


## Cloning and Running
You can clone this repo and simply execute:

```bash
git clone git://github.com/ninja-ide/ninja-ide.git
cd ninja-ide
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt
python ninja-ide.py
```

Piece of cake, huh?


## Source Code API Documentation
- [http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide](http://ninja-ide.github.io/ninja-ide "Source Code API Documentation")


## Videos
[![Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts](http://img.youtube.com/vi/xShpNY5w-64/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPopm5397ozfsS8FOSSOWGQ "Ninja-IDE Videos and Screencasts")


## License
-   **GPLv3+** *(GPLv3 or any other version later published by FSF at your option)*
